name: Bear Claws
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk, then stir in sugar, salt, and butter, allowing it to cool to lukewarm.
2. In a large bowl, combine water and yeast, stirring until dissolved. Add the milk mixture and egg. Gradually add half the flour, beating until smooth. Stir in the remaining flour to form a stiff batter. Adjust with more flour if needed. Cover tightly and refrigerate dough at least 2 hours; it may be refrigerated up to 3 days.
3. Divide the dough into two halves. Roll out one half on a lightly floured surface to a 9x18-inch rectangle. Brush with half the melted butter, then sprinkle with half the cherry mixture over the dough. Pat lightly.
4. Starting with an 18-inch edge, fold about 1/3 of the dough to the center. Fold the remaining 2/3 of the dough over to form a 3-layer strip. Pinch the edges to seal.
5. Cut each strip into 8 sections. Make 4 slashes through the cut edge of each section.
6. Place the rolls on a greased baking sheet and shape them into bear claws by separating the slashes. Repeat with the remaining dough and cherry mixture.
7. In a small bowl, blend the egg yolk and water. Brush onto the rolls.
8. Sprinkle with the remaining 1/3 cup nuts and 2 tablespoons sugar. Let rise for about 10 minutes before baking until golden brown.
CHERRY MIXTURE: Prepare this while the Sweet Rich Dough is rising. Drain and chop the cherries, then drain again on paper towels. Combine chopped cherries, dates, raisins, lemon peel, half a cup walnuts, and 2 tablespoons sugar.
